subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 4c — heatmap intensity to stock bg-primary/N
body:
Deletes the 5 .heatmap-0..4 custom CSS classes from styles.css and
replaces them with a JS constant palette in sections/Timing.jsx that
uses stock Tailwind utilities tracking DaisyUI's --color-primary.

dashboard/js/sections/Timing.jsx:
  const HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES = [
    'bg-base-300',    // level 0 — no activity
    'bg-primary/20',  // level 1
    'bg-primary/40',  // level 2
    'bg-primary/60',  // level 3
    'bg-primary',     // level 4 — max activity
  ];

  className={`... ${HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES[level]}`}

Tailwind's content scanner picks up the 5 string literals from the
array and generates the utility rules without needing a safelist.

Removed from styles.css:
  .heatmap-0 { background-color: var(--color-base-300); }
  .heatmap-1..4 { color-mix(primary 15/35/60%) + color-primary-content }
  The --chart-accent-override URL embed hook (will be fully removed
  in Phase 2 alongside chartColors.js URL params)

Removed from daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs:
  - heatmap-0 through heatmap-4 from LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist
  - "Heatmap CSS uses color-mix" regression test replaced with a new
    assertion that Timing.jsx uses HEATMAP_LEVEL_CLASSES and that
    styles.css does NOT re-introduce the deleted .heatmap-N rules

Visual difference:
  Previous gradient used color-mix 15/35/60/100% opacity over primary.
  New gradient uses Tailwind alpha 20/40/60/100% over primary.
  Very close visual match; slight saturation shift on levels 2-3.

Verified: vite build clean (3.11s), 61/61 tests pass, new test
enforces the migration.

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
